vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1190: Add post-merge UAT phase — merge ≠ done, close-out is a deliberate operator step

plan.title: Add post-merge UAT phase: merge ≠ done, close-out is a deliberate operator step
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Today's postMergeLifecycle conflates 'PR merged' with 'issue done'. The moment the operator clicks Merge, the work agent is killed, ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/ is wiped, the review-status entry is dropped, the vBRIEF is moved to completed, and the GitHub issue is auto-closed — all before the operator has even pulled main to run the merged code. Tonight (PAN-1170, 2026-05-18 12:38Z) the issue vanished from Command Deck within 17 seconds of the merge click, leaving zero window for operator UAT. Merge ≠ verified-working-on-main, and the pipeline should not pretend they are the same event.
narratives.Proposal: Split today's monolithic postMergeLifecycle into two operator-gated phases. (1) Merge handoff (postMergeLifecycle, refactored): verifies the PR landed, sets mergeStatus='merged', transitions canonical status to a new 'verifying_on_main' state, applies the 'verifying-on-main' label on GitHub, pauses (does NOT destroy) the work agent + state dir, stops Docker, and kills resource-leaky specialist sessions. Issue stays OPEN and visible on Command Deck with a distinct VERIFYING badge. (2) Close-out ceremony (existing closeOut workflow in src/lib/lifecycle/workflows.ts, extended): triggered when the operator clicks 'Close Out' on the issue (or runs `pan close <id>`). Does today's destructive cleanup — moves PRD, transitions vBRIEF active→completed, tears down workspace, deletes branches per config, prunes checkpoint refs, closes the issue on the tracker, applies 'closed-out' label, clears review status. Optional auto close-out timer for teams that don't want the manual step (default off). The change is forward-only: already-merged-and-closed issues are not migrated.

autoDecisions.summary: Use 'verifying_on_main' as the new CanonicalState (snake_case) and 'verifying-on-main' as the GitHub label (kebab-case).
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the existing canonical-state naming pattern (in_progress, in_review) while keeping the label kebab-case to match GitHub label conventions. Issue body uses 'verifying-on-main' verbatim for the label.

autoDecisions.summary: Display the new state as a 'VERIFYING' VerbBadge variant. If PAN-1148's VerbBadge component is not yet merged on main, add the equivalent inline style in WorkspaceStatusOverview.tsx + KanbanBoard.tsx so the badge ships independently.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body says 'use the VerbBadge VERIFYING from PAN-1148 or add a new variant'. We don't want to block PAN-1190 on PAN-1148's merge order — graceful fallback ensures shippability.

autoDecisions.summary: Pause the work agent at merge (set state.paused=true via setAgentPaused() in src/lib/agents.ts, kill tmux session) but preserve ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/. Operator can `pan unpause <id>` if a regression surfaces.
autoDecisions.rationale: The existing pause primitive already encodes this exact semantic. State dir preservation is the operator-resumability requirement from the issue body.

autoDecisions.summary: Stop Docker stack at merge (resource leak prevention), but preserve the workspace directory. Operators can restart Docker on demand if they want to run the workspace.
autoDecisions.rationale: Docker network pool exhaustion (the CRITICAL CLAUDE.md rule from PAN-925) requires container/network shutdown at merge regardless of operator UAT. Workspace dir survives for archaeology / re-spawn.

autoDecisions.summary: Kill reviewer/synthesis specialist sessions at merge, NOT at close-out.
autoDecisions.rationale: These sessions hold large MCP processes and Claude context — keeping them alive across a multi-hour operator UAT window has zero context-recovery value but a non-trivial RSS cost. Today's behavior already kills them at merge; preserve that.

autoDecisions.summary: Keep vBRIEF active→completed transition AT close-out (not at merge). During verify phase, vBRIEF stays at 'running' / 'active'.
autoDecisions.rationale: The canonical lifecycle (draft → proposed → approved → active/running → completed) treats 'completed' as the post-close-out terminal state. PAN-1124 single-spec-on-main rules require atomic single-commit status updates — driving the spec to 'completed' at merge would be premature if the operator reopens the issue from a regression.

autoDecisions.summary: Default close_out.remove_workspace=false and close_out.delete_feature_branch=false (operator opt-in to destruction).
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body explicitly specifies defaults are false. Conservative defaults preserve archaeology by default.

autoDecisions.summary: Default close_out.auto=false; close_out.auto_delay_minutes=60 when auto is enabled.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body explicitly specifies manual close-out is the default. 60-minute delay matches the issue body and is a reasonable UAT window for most teams.

autoDecisions.summary: Recognize the legacy 'needs-close-out' label in mapGitHubStateToCanonical as equivalent to 'verifying-on-main' for backward compat with already-merged issues that still wear the old label.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body says 'Migrating already-merged-and-closed issues into a verifying state retroactively' is OUT of scope, but issues currently OPEN with the 'needs-close-out' label (mid-flight) must not fall off the dashboard. Recognizing the legacy label as the new canonical state is a zero-cost back-compat path.

autoDecisions.summary: Auto-close-out poller lives in the existing Deacon patrol loop, not a new daemon.
autoDecisions.rationale: Deacon already runs periodic patrols (recoverOrphanedAgents, etc.) and has access to review-status timestamps. Adding a new daemon would violate the single-deacon invariant (.claude/rules/single-deacon-invariant.md).

items.title: Add 'verifying_on_main' canonical state and GitHub label mapping

narrative.Action: Extend src/core/state-mapping.ts to recognize a new canonical state between in_review and done. This is the foundation every downstream bead reads from — the state name, label spelling, and mapping precedence must be settled before postMergeLifecycle is refactored.

subItems.title: CanonicalState union in src/core/state-mapping.ts includes 'verifying_on_main' (snake_case).
CANONICAL_STATES array includes verifying_on_main with type='started' and a distinct color (suggest amber/yellow #f59e0b).
STATE_TYPE_MAP maps verifying_on_main → 'started'.

GitHub trackers stateMap: verifying_on_main → { status: 'open', label: 'verifying-on-main' }. Linear/GitLab/Jira/Trello fall back to existing in_progress/in_review semantics with auto_create when supported.
WORKFLOW_LABELS array includes 'verifying-on-main' (and keeps 'needs-close-out' for back-compat).
getStateLabel(verifying_on_main) returns 'verifying-on-main'.

mapGitHubStateToCanonical: an OPEN issue with the 'verifying-on-main' label OR the legacy 'needs-close-out' label maps to verifying_on_main (NOT done). Precedence: verifying-on-main beats merged-without-close-out semantics — issue stays in verifying column until close-out applies 'closed-out' and closes the issue.

CLOSED GitHub issues with 'closed-out' label still map to 'done' (close-out terminal). CLOSED issues without 'closed-out' but with 'merged' or 'verifying-on-main' also map to 'done' (treat closed-without-ceremony as terminal).
Unit tests in src/core/__tests__ (or co-located test file) cover the new state, the new label precedence, the legacy 'needs-close-out' back-compat path, and the closed-issue terminal path.

items.title: Wire the new canonical state through frontend Issue / ReviewStatus types

narrative.Action: Update src/dashboard/frontend/src/types.ts and any contracts in packages/contracts to surface the new canonical state to React components. Do NOT add a new mergeStatus value — the existing 'merged' value plus the canonical state on the Issue is sufficient.

subItems.title: Issue.state TypeScript type union in src/dashboard/frontend/src/types.ts includes 'verifying_on_main' (and matches the canonical state).
If packages/contracts has a CanonicalState schema, it is updated and the contract package rebuilt (npm run build inside packages/contracts).
npm run typecheck passes from the workspace root after the type additions.

items.title: Extend closeOut() workflow to absorb steps moving out of postMergeLifecycle

narrative.Action: Before postMergeLifecycle stops doing destructive work (next bead), enhance closeOut() in src/lib/lifecycle/workflows.ts so it picks up the operations currently done at merge time. The vBRIEF transition, checkpoint-ref prune, and 'verifying-on-main' label removal must all live in closeOut by the end of this bead.

subItems.title: closeOut() runs a vBRIEF active→completed transition via transitionVBriefOnMain (port from merge-agent.ts step 5c, between archive and teardown).
closeOut() prunes checkpoint refs for agent-<id> and planning-<id> via pruneCheckpointRefsForAgents (port from merge-agent.ts step 7b).
closeOut()'s closeIssue step applies 'closed-out' label AND removes 'verifying-on-main' + legacy 'needs-close-out' labels in a single GitHub edit.
teardownWorkspace inside closeOut respects new config flags (next bead introduces them): deleteWorkspace and deleteBranches default false; both can be flipped via cloister config.
Existing closeOut step ordering (verify-merged → archive → teardown → close → label → clear-status) is preserved; new steps slot in as additive non-mutating phases.
WorkflowResult.steps now reports the new vBRIEF and checkpoint-ref steps with stepOk/stepSkipped semantics.
Unit test exercises closeOut against a fixture workspace and asserts the new steps appear in WorkflowResult.steps.

items.title: Refactor postMergeLifecycle into a non-destructive merge-handoff

narrative.Action: Surgically refactor postMergeLifecycle() in src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts so it stops being the destructive end-of-life event. After this bead the function MUST leave the workspace, agent state dir, vBRIEF, feature branch, and GitHub issue intact, transitioning the issue to canonical state verifying_on_main. The previously-destructive steps now live in closeOut (previous bead).

subItems.title: postMergeLifecycle still runs: merge verification, setReviewStatus({mergeStatus:'merged'}), stash drop, server deploy script handoff, Docker container/network shutdown, reviewer/synthesis session kill, specialist tmux session kill (review/test/merge), beads compaction, TLDR daemon reindex notification, slot-branch swarm handoff.

postMergeLifecycle NO LONGER runs: closeIssueWithCircuitBreaker, vBRIEF active→completed transition, agent state dir removal, workspace teardown, feature-branch deletion, PRD active→completed move, checkpoint-ref prune. closeIssueWithCircuitBreaker and its tracking state may be deleted if no other caller remains.

Work agent + planning agent are PAUSED (not deleted): setAgentPaused(agentId, 'awaiting close-out (verify on main)', true) is called for each. tmux sessions are killed (resource leak prevention), but ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/state.json survives with paused=true.

Canonical state transition: a new helper writes 'verifying_on_main' to the issue's canonical state surface (issue-lifecycle.ts label rules + tracker label edit). On GitHub: add 'verifying-on-main' label, remove 'in-review' / 'in-progress'. Do NOT close the GitHub issue. The legacy 'needs-close-out' label is no longer applied here (replaced by 'verifying-on-main').
Idempotency guards (_completedPostMerge Set, _postMergeInFlight) still work but their semantics now mean 'verify-handoff already done' rather than 'issue cleaned up'.
resetPostMergeState(issueId) is callable from the close-out flow so close-out can permit re-entry if a regression triggers a reopen.

After the handoff, the issue is observable on the dashboard as: GitHub state=open, labels include 'verifying-on-main', review-status mergeStatus='merged', work agent state.json paused=true, workspace dir present, vBRIEF still at 'active' / 'running', GitHub issue still OPEN.
Existing test src/lib/cloister/__tests__/merge-agent-stash.test.ts (and any other merge-agent tests) still pass; new test asserts the non-destructive contract above.
activity log entry on completion reads 'Merged <id>. Awaiting close-out (verify on main).' (not 'Issue moved to Done').

narrative.Action: Add a dashboard HTTP route the 'Close Out' button (next bead) hits. The route calls closeOut() from src/lib/lifecycle/workflows.ts and returns the WorkflowResult. Register the route alongside the existing issues routes in src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts.

subItems.title: POST /api/issues/:id/close-out endpoint registered in src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts via the Effect HttpRouter.add pattern used elsewhere in the file.
Route resolves the project from the issue ID (resolveProjectFromIssue), builds a LifecycleContext (issueId, projectPath, github=…), and invokes closeOut(ctx) from src/lib/lifecycle/index.ts.

Route returns 200 + JSON WorkflowResult on success. On failure (verify-merged step failed, or closeOut returned success=false) returns 422 with the failed step details. On project resolution failure returns 404. On unhandled exception returns 500 with a sanitized message.
Route uses async fs / promisified exec only (no execSync, no readFileSync) — complies with .claude/rules/no-execsync-server.md.
Route uses internal token guard (INTERNAL_TOKEN_HEADER) if other state-mutating dashboard endpoints do.
Route is idempotent: calling close-out twice on the same issue returns success the second time (no-op when issue already closed).

items.title: Add VERIFYING badge and Close Out button to dashboard issue surfaces

narrative.Action: Surface the new verifying-on-main state in the dashboard. Add a 'VERIFYING' badge wherever issue status badges render (KanbanBoard cards, InspectorPanel, DetailPanelLayout, WorkspaceStatusOverview) and a 'Close Out' button to the issue action bar (KanbanBoard card actions, InspectorPanel, DetailPanelLayout) that POSTs to the new close-out route. Use the VerbBadge component if it exists in main (PAN-1148); otherwise add a styled inline equivalent.

subItems.title: When issue.state === 'verifying_on_main', a 'VERIFYING' badge appears on the KanbanBoard card, InspectorPanel header, and DetailPanelLayout header.

The VERIFYING badge is visually distinct from the existing transient mergeStatus='verifying' (merge-in-flight) badge — different color or shape so operators can tell the post-merge state apart from the pre-merge progress state.
A 'Close Out' button appears on issue action surfaces when issue.state === 'verifying_on_main'. The button is hidden otherwise.

Clicking Close Out POSTs to /api/issues/:id/close-out, shows a confirmation modal listing destructive steps, awaits the response, and on success removes the issue from the current view via React Query invalidation.
Existing Merge button is hidden when issue.state === 'verifying_on_main' (merge already happened).
If PAN-1148's VerbBadge component is present, add 'VERIFYING' as a variant of that component; otherwise create the badge inline and leave a `// TODO(PAN-1148): consolidate into VerbBadge` comment.
Playwright e2e test (isolated browser instance/profile per test) drives the flow: merge → badge appears → click Close Out → confirmation modal → success → issue gone from the column.

items.title: Keep verifying issues visible on Command Deck and Pipeline; remove from Awaiting Merge

narrative.Action: Verify the queries that drive Command Deck (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ZoneCOverviewTabs/queries.ts), KanbanBoard, and Pipeline include verifying_on_main as a visible state. Verify Awaiting Merge / readyForMerge filters EXCLUDE verifying issues. Add an 'Awaiting Close-Out' phase surface if Pipeline groups by phase.

subItems.title: Command Deck Zone C (Overview) lists verifying_on_main issues alongside in_progress/in_review issues (not hidden as if 'done').
Awaiting Merge query/filter excludes issues with mergeStatus='merged' OR canonical state='verifying_on_main' (these are past merge).

KanbanBoard maps verifying_on_main into a sensible column — either a new 'Verifying' column or fold into 'Done' as a non-default sub-state with badge. Choose: a new 'Verifying' column (rationale recorded in continue.json) between Review and Done.
Pipeline phase grouping (usePipelinePhase.ts) treats verifying_on_main as a phase between 'merged' and any terminal state; the phase enum gains 'verifying' if not present.
Removing the Awaiting Merge row for a freshly-merged issue happens reactively via React Query invalidation, not a full page reload.

items.title: Update Deacon reconcile paths to recognize the new non-destructive postMergeLifecycle

narrative.Action: Walk each Deacon call site of postMergeLifecycle in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts (lines ~2481, ~3135, ~3903 today) and confirm the new non-destructive contract is acceptable. Deacon should NOT trigger auto close-out from these paths — that's a separate bead. Update orphan-recovery and reconcile sweeps so a paused work agent for a verifying-on-main issue is recognized as 'expected paused', not 'stopped, needs auto-resume'.

subItems.title: autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents() (or equivalent) skips agents whose state.json has paused=true AND mergeStatus='merged' (verify phase) — never auto-resumes them.

All Deacon paths that historically called postMergeLifecycle (reconcileMergedIssues, gh-reconcile, processPendingLifecycle) still call the refactored function; the call shape and arguments are unchanged.
recoverOrphanedAgents does NOT treat a paused-for-verify agent as orphaned — it's an expected steady state, not a recovery target.
Deacon log lines distinguish 'verify-paused' from 'manually-paused' in the agent inventory so operators reading logs aren't confused.

items.title: Add close_out section to CloisterConfig with workspace / branch / auto knobs

narrative.Action: Add a new optional close_out config section to CloisterConfig in src/lib/cloister/config.ts with the four flags the issue body specifies. Wire the flags into closeOut() so teardownWorkspace receives the correct deleteWorkspace / deleteBranches values.

subItems.title: CloisterConfig.close_out is an optional object with fields: remove_workspace: boolean (default false), delete_feature_branch: boolean (default false), auto: boolean (default false), auto_delay_minutes: number (default 60).
DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG includes a close_out block populated with the defaults above (omitting the field is equivalent to default-false / default-60).
closeOut() in src/lib/lifecycle/workflows.ts reads the config flags via the existing loadCloisterConfig (or equivalent) helper and passes them to teardownWorkspace.
JSON schema or zod schema (whichever the rest of the config uses) updated to validate the new section.
projects.yaml example/comment documents the new section so operators see the knobs in their actual config file.

items.title: Optional auto-closeout Deacon patrol when close_out.auto=true

narrative.Action: Add a Deacon patrol that, when close_out.auto=true, finds issues in verifying_on_main older than close_out.auto_delay_minutes (measured from review-status mergeStatus=merged timestamp) and runs closeOut() against them. Default config leaves this disabled — operators opt in.

subItems.title: Deacon patrols include an autoCloseOut() sweep that runs on the existing patrol cadence (no new daemon, no new socket).
Sweep is gated on close_out.auto === true; when false the sweep returns immediately with zero work and zero log output.
Eligibility filter: review-status entries with mergeStatus='merged' AND canonical state === 'verifying_on_main' AND updatedAt older than (now - close_out.auto_delay_minutes).
Each eligible issue runs closeOut(ctx) with auto: true context flag so log lines / activity entries distinguish auto-close-out from operator-driven close-out.
Sweep is idempotent — a successful closeOut clears the canonical state from verifying_on_main, so the next sweep skips the issue.

If closeOut fails, the sweep does NOT retry in a tight loop — it backs off (re-eligible no sooner than auto_delay_minutes later) and surfaces the failure via emitActivityEntry so an operator can intervene.

items.title: Tighten pan close CLI to validate verifying-on-main state and update skill

narrative.Action: `pan close <id>` already drives closeOut(). Tighten the CLI to warn (not block, unless --force-anyway) when the issue is not in verifying_on_main state, and update the pan-close skill documentation to reflect the new operator flow.

subItems.title: pan close <id> reads the canonical state and prints a yellow warning + confirmation prompt if state !== 'verifying_on_main'. --force skips the prompt.
Confirmation step output mentions that the issue should normally be in 'verifying-on-main' before close-out.
skills/pan-close/SKILL.md describes the merge → verify-on-main → close-out flow (replacing any 'merge auto-closes' wording).
scripts/lint-skills.sh continues to pass (no orphan flags in SKILL.md vs pan close --help output).

items.title: Make reopen-from-Done restore the right canonical state

narrative.Action: If a closed-out issue is reopened on the tracker (gh issue reopen / Linear state Done→In Progress), the pipeline must restore a sensible canonical state. mapGitHubStateToCanonical already handles open issues by label — extend it so a reopened issue with 'verifying-on-main' label maps back to verifying_on_main, and so reopening clears the 'closed-out' label.

subItems.title: mapGitHubStateToCanonical: an OPEN issue with the 'closed-out' label maps to in_progress (clean-up state — labels are stale after reopen), NOT done.
issue-lifecycle's open transition removes 'closed-out' along with the other workflow labels.
If review-status still has mergeStatus='merged' when an issue is reopened, the pipeline restores canonical state verifying_on_main (operator wants to UAT again after a regression fix landed).
Unit tests cover: closed→reopened with 'closed-out' label → in_progress; merged+verifying-on-main label on OPEN issue → verifying_on_main; closed with 'closed-out' → done.

items.title: End-to-end and unit tests for the verify → close-out flow

narrative.Action: Add tests that exercise the new lifecycle from merge through close-out, including the non-destructive contract of postMergeLifecycle and the operator path through closeOut. Playwright e2e uses an isolated browser instance per test.

subItems.title: Unit test asserts postMergeLifecycle preserves workspace dir, agent state dir, GitHub issue OPEN, and vBRIEF active status after running against a fixture.
Unit test asserts closeOut() destroys workspace dir per config flag, transitions vBRIEF to completed, applies 'closed-out' label, removes 'verifying-on-main' label, closes GitHub issue.
Integration test: POST /api/issues/:id/close-out against a verifying-on-main fixture returns 200 + WorkflowResult with success=true.
Playwright e2e test runs in an isolated browser instance/profile (no shared state with other tests), drives merge → VERIFYING badge → Close Out button → success → issue removed from board.
All new tests pass under `npm test` from the workspace root.

items.title: Update docs/ROLES.md, CLAUDE.md, and pan-close docs for the new verify phase

narrative.Action: Document the new canonical state, the merge/verify/close-out split, and the configuration knobs. Touch docs/ROLES.md, CLAUDE.md (the postMergeLifecycle Docker cleanup section still applies — update wording to reflect close-out destination for non-Docker steps), and skills/pan-close/SKILL.md.

subItems.title: docs/ROLES.md gains a short section on the verifying_on_main state and what each role does (or does not do) during it.
CLAUDE.md postMergeLifecycle Docker cleanup section is updated: Docker stop remains at merge; vBRIEF transition / workspace teardown / issue close are explicitly moved to close-out.
skills/pan-close/SKILL.md describes the new flow and lists the close_out config flags.
If docs/PIPELINE.md or similar exists, it includes the verifying phase in the diagram.
